source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
There are 10 boxes in the first van. Every further van contains twice as many boxes as the previous one. How many boxes are there in the fifth van?
<image1>

image_dependency: question_text is fully understandable as text-only arithmetic
although image_manifest contains 1 PNG, the question does not reference any visual content
multi_step: the problem requires using the initial value in the first van and the rule that each further van has twice the previous van
solving needs repeated application of the doubling relation up to the fifth van or an equivalent geometric-sequence computation
the final answer is not directly stated and must be derived from multiple linked steps
verifiability: answer_text='160' is a precise single-value target
the computation can be checked deterministically from the text rule

decision_reasons: Question text and numeric answer are both present, so the core semantic assets are intact.
Initial scoring indicates the problem is fully understandable from text and has very high verifiability.
The sample appears solvable and checkable without relying on ambiguous visual content.
The only flagged issue is a metadata image path mismatch, which is a manageable registry inconsistency rather than a loss of core problem content.
